lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The Bodleian, Oxford. A room of locked chains and catalogued silence.

lines.speaker: Mele
lines.text: But who actually got in? Not who was meant to. Who walked through.

lines.speaker: Sor Juana
lines.text: Not women, as a rule. I read in a convent because the university was not mine to enter.

lines.speaker: Gottfried
lines.text: Nor the poor, however clever. You needed a patron's letter, a college affiliation, a name someone recognised.

lines.speaker: Mele
lines.text: So the argument about what to keep was always also an argument about who was permitted to need it.

lines.speaker: Sor Juana
lines.text: Every library has two collections. What is on the shelves, and who is allowed to reach them.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Access, it turns out, is not a footnote to the history of knowledge. It is the oldest hidden argument inside it.
